title: Rolling Mirror Bathroom Wall Cabinet
summary: Elevate a simple wall cabinet into something amazing by swapping traditional doors for rolling round mirrors.
description: Elevate a simple wall cabinet into something amazing by swapping traditional doors for rolling round mirrors. Use this cabinet for a unique medicine cabinet, vanity mirror, or over the toilet organization. A perfect, modern storage solution for any room.

steps.title: Cut boards and Gooves
steps.body:
Following the cut diagram, use a Kreg Accu-Cut and circular saw or table saw to cut your box pieces to size from a 1 x 12 x 96" and a 1 x 6 x 36" board.
Cut two at 8-1/4" x 28" Cut two at 9" x 18-5/8" Cut one at 5-1/2" x 28"
Grooves need to be cut into the top, bottom and sides to accommodate a 1/4" inset plywood panel. This can be done by taking multiple passes using the Kreg Accu-Cut guide track and a circular saw, on a table saw, or by using a router and 1/4" straight cutting bit.
Cut 1/4" x 1/4" grooves along the length of the side pieces, 3-1/4" from the front edge of the board. Cut a 1/4" x 1/4" groove along the length of the top and bottom pieces, but stop 1/4" from either end. Do not cut through the ends of the board.

steps.title: Assemble Mirror Frames
steps.body:
Cut 12 trapezoids from 1 x 4 boards, 30 degrees off of square, the longest side measuring 10-1/2".
For each frame: Align 6 trapezoids in a hexagon shape. Measure the exact length between two parallel pieces and cut a scrap piece of wood to fit between them. Apply glue to all adjoining faces and clamp together to create a hexagon.
Allow to dry overnight.

steps.title: Cut Mirror Frames
steps.body: Using a router and circle jig, bandsaw, or jigsaw, cut a 3/4" wide ring with an outer diameter of 17" and an inner diameter of 15-1/2".

steps.title: Cut Rabbet
steps.body: Use a router to cut a 3/8" wide by 1/4" deep rabbet on the inside rim of the mirror frame ring.

steps.title: Add Mirror to Frame
steps.body: Sand and apply finish to both mirror frame rings and allow to dry. Inside each each frame, place a 16" diameter, 1/8" thick mirror within the rabbet. Secure in place using hot glue around edges.

steps.title: Cut Plywood Panel
steps.body: Use a Kreg Accu-Cut track and circular saw to cut a 17-5/8" x 28-1/2" panel from 1/4" plywood or MDF. Measure 1-1/4" from both short sides and 1" from both long sides. Use a jigsaw to cut two, intersecting, 15" diameter circles.

steps.title: Insert Panel Into Sides
steps.body: Slide the plywood panel into 1/4" grooves of side boards.

steps.title: Insert Panel Into Top & Bottom
steps.body: Align the plywood panel inside the grooves of the top and bottom boards. Join top and bottom boards to sides using glue and 1-1/4" screws though 3/4" pocket holes.

steps.title: Add Shelf
steps.body:
On the inside of the box, align the shelf 8-1/4" from the bottom board. Secure in place using glue and 1-1/4" screws through 3/4" pocket holes.
Sand and apply finish to cabinet box, unattached face frame pieces and center rails.

steps.title: Place First Mirror
steps.body: Place first mirror flush against the front face of the plywood panel.

steps.title: Add Center Rails
steps.body: Place 3/4" x 3/4" x 28" center rails against top and bottom boards, spaced 1-1/2" from the front edge. Secure in place using glue and 1-1/4" brads.

steps.title: Place Second Mirror
steps.body: Place the second mirror in place in front of the center rails.

steps.title: Attach Face Frames
steps.body: Add 1 x 2 x 28" face frames, flush to the front edges of top and bottom boards. Attach using wood glue and 1-1/4" brad nails through the face and ends.

steps.title: Get More Tips and Tricks
steps.body: Head to www.PneumaticAddict.com for additional DIY tips, ideas, and free building plans.
